meta.title: José Fernando García Pérez — Senior Geospatial Data Engineer & Analytics Engineer
meta.description: Senior Geospatial Data Engineer & Analytics Engineer. 20+ years GIS, 6+ years analytics. Power BI, dbt, DuckDB, Python, Mapbox, FastAPI. Open to remote (USD/EUR). Medellín, Colombia.
meta.keywords: Geospatial Data Engineer, Analytics Engineer, GIS, Power BI, dbt, Python, Mapbox, FastAPI, Medellín, remote

hero.status: Open to work · Remote (USD/EUR) · Hybrid Colombia
hero.pre: Senior Geospatial Data Engineer & Analytics Engineer
hero.skills: GIS · Power BI · dbt · Python · Mapbox · SQL
hero.sub: Civil engineer with 20+ years in geospatial intelligence and 6+ years as an analytics engineer. I design medallion pipelines tested in dbt/DuckDB, Mapbox geoportals, and dashboards that survive regulatory audits.

capabilities.title: What I deliver
capabilities.sub: Three roles, one engineer. Hire me for any of the following.

items.title: Cloud data pipelines & geospatial ETL
items.desc: Production pipelines from raw spatial data to clean analytics layers. Python automation, PostGIS, ArcPy, Azure Data Factory. Built for institutional scale.

items.title: Medallion pipelines, dbt & executive dashboards
items.desc: Re-engineering legacy Power BI/DAX processes into Python → DuckDB → dbt pipelines with tests, PII anonymization, and Chart.js or Power BI dashboards with star/bridge models.

items.title: Spatial systems & national-standard databases
items.desc: Geodatabases under ANLA / IGAC standards. Multi-criteria spatial models. Hydrological networks. MAGNA-SIRGAS CTM12 reprojection. ArcGIS Pro, QGIS, PostGIS.

featured.desc: A retrieval-augmented generation system deployed in production for the City of Medellín. Lets urban-planning teams query the territorial plan in natural language and retrieve grounded answers with citations to the source document.
featured.disclaimer: Live system in production for the City of Medellín. Access credentials available on request for serious inquiries.
featured.userQuestion: ¿Cuáles son las áreas de protección ambiental establecidas en el POT?
featured.aiAnswerIntro: Según el POT vigente, las áreas de protección ambiental se clasifican en:
aiAnswerItems: 1. Áreas de retiro a corrientes hídricas
featured.previewNote: Sanitized UI preview · Actual interface includes authentication

caseStudies.gallerySub: Production dashboard screenshots. Reports with PII (AMVA permits, DAP legal) are image-only; all others can be viewed live below.
caseStudies.galleryExtrasSub: Additional Power BI report pages and web portfolios.
caseStudies.embedNote: Public transparency dashboards · Select a report to preview inline or open full screen.

amva.title: AMVA — Environmental permits pipeline
amva.summary: Power BI/DAX re-engineering to Python → DuckDB → dbt with anonymized Chart.js + Mapbox web dashboard for 3,865 metropolitan cases.
amva.challenge: AMVA needed traceable permit cycles with business-day logic, 5 SGC areas, and audit-ready KPIs — plus a PII-free public version.
amva.approach: Medallion bronze/silver/gold in dbt (13 tests), Law 1581/2012 anonymization, 206,680 task-level records. Auto-updating web dashboard with 1,068 georeferenced cases. Original Power BI documented as screenshots.
amva.outcome: Internationally shareable anonymized demo, reproducible pipeline, and less manual reporting.

medellin-dap.title: Medellín DAP — Urban planning intelligence
medellin-dap.summary: Seven Power BI dashboards, subdirectorate web portfolio, POT RAG assistant, and anonymized cadastral analytics.
medellin-dap.challenge: DAP required coordinated analytics across legal tracking, strategic themes, participation, and executive KPIs with spatial planning workflows.
medellin-dap.approach: Power Query M star/bridge models, dashboards per workstream, and ArcPy multicriteria analysis. Legal dashboard documented as screenshots due to PII; cadastral analytics with public demo.
medellin-dap.outcome: Teams monitor POT milestones, legal commitments, and participation metrics. Recruiters see anonymized demos and institutional dashboard screenshots.

rag-pot-medellin.title: RAG assistant — POT Medellín (322 pages)
rag-pot-medellin.summary: Production retrieval-augmented generation system letting planners query the territorial plan in Spanish with cited sources.
rag-pot-medellin.challenge: A 322-page legal/technical POT is impractical to search manually during meetings and revision cycles. Teams needed grounded answers, not hallucinations.
rag-pot-medellin.approach: Document chunking, vector retrieval, Spanish LLM orchestration, and citation-first response formatting. Deployed on Render with authenticated access for DAP teams.
rag-pot-medellin.outcome: ~5s average responses with page-level citations. Reduces time to locate norms on environmental protection, land use, and strategic revision topics.

caseStudies.galleryOnlyNote: Screenshot preview — institutional data not anonymized. The linked public demo uses anonymized data.

vertex-rd.title: Vertex RD — 360° cadastral geoportal
vertex-rd.summary: Full-stack geoportal for Distrito Nacional (Dominican Republic): 140,237 parcels, 22 vector layers, Parcel Sheet 360°, and Mapbox urban analytics.
vertex-rd.challenge: The client needed to query 140K parcels with zoning, density, and setback context — without desktop GIS or static reports.
vertex-rd.approach: 22 shapefiles → 10 Mapbox tilesets. FastAPI + PostGIS backend (SQLite fallback) with parcel search API. Mapbox GL JS 3.10 + Turf.js + Chart.js frontend. Docker on Render.
vertex-rd.outcome: Sub-second parcel lookup, barrio analytics integrated into the map, and successful international remote delivery in USD.

medellin-cadastral.title: Medellín Cadastral Analytics
medellin-cadastral.summary: Analytics engineering pipeline over 15.3M+ historical cadastral records (2015–2024) with dbt marts and Mapbox choropleths.
medellin-cadastral.challenge: Consolidate a decade of heterogeneous cadastral records into reliable barrio/comuna aggregates with spatial reprojection and CBML keys.
medellin-cadastral.approach: Python/GeoPandas → DuckDB → bronze/silver/gold dbt layers with tests. MAGNA-SIRGAS→WGS84 reprojection. Public Mapbox choropleth demo on GitHub Pages.
medellin-cadastral.outcome: Reproducible marts, auditable territorial metrics, and a recruiter-ready public demo.

about.title: About
about.name: José Fernando García Pérez
about.p1: Civil engineer (Universidad Nacional de Colombia, 2006) with three postgraduate specializations. Licensed under T.P. 05202139705 ANT.
about.p2: Two decades in GIS and 6+ years in data analytics. Today I combine medallion pipelines (dbt/DuckDB), Mapbox geoportals, Power BI dashboards, and production RAG assistants for government and infrastructure.
about.p3: Currently expanding into Azure, Databricks and Microsoft Fabric. Working remote, hybrid, or on-site for clients worldwide.
about.cvNote: Place your PDF at public/cv-jose-fernando-garcia.pdf to enable download.
